title: Lachha Paratha

subs.name: half atta, half maida for a more restaurant-style, flakier paratha

ingredients.name: oil or ghee, for the dough

subs.name: neutral oil for a dairy-free version

ingredients.name: ghee or oil, for layering and cooking

subs.name: neutral oil for a dairy-free version

steps.text: Mix atta, salt and 1 tbsp ghee. Add water gradually and knead into a soft, pliable dough. Cover and rest for 20 minutes — the resting is important for rolling thin without tearing.
Divide the dough into 8 balls. Roll one ball into a thin, large circle about 20 cm wide. Spread a thin, even layer of softened ghee or oil all over the surface.
Starting from one edge, make accordion pleats along the entire circle — fold about 2 cm, then fold back 2 cm, and so on, until you have a long pleated strip. The pleating creates the layers.

Coil the pleated strip tightly into a round swirl from one end to the other, tucking the end underneath. Gently flatten the coil with your palm, then roll it out carefully to a 16 cm round — do not press too hard or you will press out the layers.
Heat a tawa over medium flame. Place the paratha and cook for about 90 seconds until the underside shows light golden specks. Flip, brush ghee over the cooked side, and cook another 90 seconds.

Flip one more time, brush the second side with ghee, and cook until both sides are evenly golden with crisp edges, about 1 more minute. While still hot, use your palms to crush the paratha lightly — this separates the layers dramatically. Serve with dal makhani or paneer gravy.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: ghee → neutral oil for a dairy-free version; wheat-flour → half atta, half maida for a more restaurant-style, flakier paratha
